flashcards.back:
• Available[m]: Free resources
• Max[n×m]: Maximum demand declared
• Allocation[n×m]: Currently allocated
• Need[n×m]: Max - Allocation

n = processes, m = resource types

1. Work = Available, Finish = false
2. Find i: Finish[i]=false AND Need[i]≤Work
3. If found: Work += Allocation[i], Finish[i]=true, goto 2
4. All Finish=true → SAFE

Returns safe sequence if exists

1. Request > Need? → Error!
2. Request > Available? → Wait
3. Pretend allocate:
   Available -= Request
   Allocation += Request
   Need -= Request
4. Safety check: Safe→Grant, Unsafe→Deny & restore

• Max claims required upfront
• Fixed processes and resources
• O(mn²) overhead per request
• Conservative (may deny safe requests)
• Processes must return resources
• Not practical for real-time systems
